question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TopicMessageFilter with limit set to a value near Long.MAX_VALUE so that the cast `(int) (filter.getLimit() - context.getTotal().get())` overflows to a negative integer, causing pageSize to be computed as Math.min(negative, maxPageSize) and resulting in a zero or negative DB query limit that silently skips fee-schedule topic messages, effectively freezing the mirror node on stale transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user set limit=0 in TopicMessageFilter so that hasLimit() returns false, causing the poll() method to use Integer.MAX_VALUE as the effective limit and issue an unbounded database query against the fee-schedule topic, exhausting DB connection pool resources and denying fee-schedule updates to all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a startTime of 0 in TopicMessageFilter to force the retriever to scan the entire history of a fee-schedule topic from genesis, causing the poll() method to issue a query returning millions of rows, exhausting heap memory and preventing current fee-schedule messages from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: PollingContext(TopicMessageFilter, boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the unthrottled code path (throttled=false) by exploiting the safety-check gap-recovery flow in TopicMessageServiceImpl, causing numRepeats to be set to unthrottled.getMaxPolls() and frequency to 20ms, resulting in rapid-fire DB polling against fee-schedule topics that starves other connections and delays fee-schedule ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a subscription where pageSize.get() always equals retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize() by ensuring exactly maxPageSize messages exist in the fee-schedule topic, causing isComplete() to always return false in throttled mode and keeping the retriever in an infinite polling loop that monopolizes DB threads?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the live read of retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize() inside isComplete() versus the snapshot of maxPageSize captured at PollingContext construction time, creating a discrepancy where the completion check uses a different page size than the query, causing the retriever to never terminate and continuously re-query fee-schedule topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: onNext(TopicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition between the non-atomic sequence of last.set(topicMessage), total.incrementAndGet(), and pageSize.incrementAndGet() in onNext() by opening many concurrent subscriptions to a fee-schedule topic, causing total and pageSize to diverge and the limit calculation in poll() to produce incorrect values that skip or duplicate fee-schedule messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a TopicMessageFilter with a consensusTimestamp-derived startTime such that last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 overflows Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the next poll's startTime to wrap to Long.MIN_VALUE and re-retrieve all historical fee-schedule messages from the beginning, leading to fee-schedule replay and incorrect fee state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of simultaneous gRPC subscriptions to a fee-schedule topic with startTime=0 and no limit, causing poll() to issue thousands of concurrent full-table scans via topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter), exhausting the DB connection pool and preventing the importer from writing new fee-schedule updates?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: PollingContext(TopicMessageFilter, boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user force the throttled=true path by subscribing normally, causing numRepeats to be set to Long.MAX_VALUE and keeping the polling loop alive indefinitely, so that a fee-schedule topic subscription never terminates and accumulates server-side resources proportional to the number of such subscriptions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user set limit=1 in TopicMessageFilter targeting a fee-schedule topic, causing poll() to compute pageSize=1 and issue single-row queries in a tight loop at the unthrottled polling frequency of 20ms, generating excessive DB load that delays processing of fee-schedule updates for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that context.getPageSize().set(0L) resets the page size counter before each poll without synchronization, causing a concurrent onNext() call to increment pageSize after the reset but before the query completes, leading to an off-by-one in isComplete() that causes one extra poll fetching duplicate fee-schedule messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a filter with hasLimit()=true and limit exactly equal to the number of fee-schedule messages in the DB, so that limitHit becomes true and isComplete() returns true prematurely in unthrottled mode, causing the retriever to stop before a concurrently-written fee-schedule update is returned, leaving the subscriber with stale fee data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a TopicMessageFilter with endTime set to a value just before the latest fee-schedule message's consensusTimestamp, causing the rebuilt filter in poll() to exclude the most recent fee-schedule update and return an outdated fee schedule to the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user set startTime to a future nanosecond timestamp just below the validation boundary in TopicMessageFilter.isValidStartTime(), causing poll() to build a newFilter with that future startTime and return zero results indefinitely, making the subscriber believe no fee-schedule messages exist and causing it to operate on default/zero fees?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: PollingContext(TopicMessageFilter, boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the unthrottled path with maxPolls=12 and pollingFrequency=20ms, then time their subscription to a fee-schedule topic so that all 12 polls complete before the latest fee-schedule message is committed to the DB, causing the retriever to complete without ever returning the current fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.fromStream(topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter)) call by subscribing and immediately cancelling the reactive stream, causing the underlying JDBC ResultSet or stream to be left open, leaking DB connections and eventually preventing fee-schedule topic queries from executing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a TopicMessageFilter with a very large limit (e.g., Integer.MAX_VALUE) so that the cast `(int) (filter.getLimit() - context.getTotal().get())` produces Integer.MAX_VALUE as pageSize, bypassing the Math.min(limit, context.getMaxPageSize()) guard and causing the DB query to fetch up to Integer.MAX_VALUE rows of fee-schedule messages in a single call?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: onComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a subscription that emits a very large total count, causing the rate calculation `(int) (1000.0 * total.get() / elapsed)` in onComplete() to overflow an int, producing a garbage log entry that obscures monitoring of fee-schedule retrieval throughput and hides anomalous access patterns?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with throttled=true (numRepeats=Long.MAX_VALUE) to a fee-schedule topic and never cancel it, causing the server to maintain an infinite polling loop that continuously queries the DB, incrementally increasing server load until fee-schedule processing latency degrades for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: PollingContext(TopicMessageFilter, boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxPageSize is captured once at PollingContext construction from retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize() but isComplete() re-reads retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize() live, so that if the property is updated between construction and completion check, the retriever may loop extra times fetching already-processed fee-schedule messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a TopicMessageFilter targeting a non-fee-schedule topic ID that happens to have the same numeric ID as a fee-schedule topic in a different shard/realm, causing poll() to retrieve messages from the wrong topic and feed incorrect fee data to the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user set limit to exactly Integer.MAX_VALUE + 1 (as a long) in TopicMessageFilter, causing the narrowing cast `(int) (filter.getLimit() - context.getTotal().get())` to produce -1 when total=0, which Math.min(-1, maxPageSize) resolves to -1, and the DB query with limit=-1 either throws an exception or returns all rows, bypassing the maxPageSize design constraint?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the throttled branch of isComplete() where `pageSize.get() < retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize()` is evaluated, by timing their subscription so that exactly maxPageSize messages are returned in each poll, keeping isComplete() returning false indefinitely and causing the retriever to loop forever on a fee-schedule topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll(PollingContext)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with startTime set to the exact consensusTimestamp of a known fee-schedule message, causing poll() to include that message in the first page and then set startTime to consensusTimestamp+1 for subsequent polls, but if the fee-schedule message is the only one, the retriever terminates after one poll and the subscriber never receives future fee-schedule updates?
